summary: Iteration 4 testing of CardCloud complete. Backend: 14/15 new pytest cases pass (1 acceptable skip on AI estimate when Claude returns non-JSON/502). Full iter 1-3 regression 36/36 green. Frontend: Playwright verified share create/copy/open/revoke flow from card 3-dot menu (badge appears + 'Share link revoked' toast on revoke), Watchlist View-sold-comps eBay link + AI estimate button (clicked, returned real Claude JSON '$180–$280 typical $225' for Luka Doncic Prizm Silver RC, persisted and rendered in ai-estimate box), Profile public vault toggle ON produces public-vault-link + copy/open buttons, /s/v/<token> page loaded unauthenticated showing 6 cards with NO cost/sales labels, /s/c/<token> page loaded with image + tags and NO leaked price_paid/price_sold/expenses/where_bought fields, bad share token shows 'LINK NOT FOUND'. Multi-image endpoints verified: POST replace=false appends, PUT /primary-image swaps old primary into images[], DELETE ?path=X removes specific image or clears primary, 400 on unknown path. /api/public/image correctly serves authorized paths and returns 404 for unrelated paths.

minor.issue: EMERGENT_LLM_KEY hit budget limit mid-test (LiteLLM: 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.001755, Max budget: 0.001'); endpoint correctly returned 502 as spec allows. Frontend test run before budget exhaustion produced valid JSON ($180-$280 typical $225), confirming integration works end-to-end.

issues: Recharts still emits width(-1)/height(-1) warnings on initial mount (carried from iter 2, benign)

action_items: None blocking. Optional: top-up EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget (currently at $0.001 cap) for continued AI estimate usage; integration itself is correct.
Optional (carried): split server.py (~1150 lines now) into feature routers.
Optional (carried): wrap recharts ResponsiveContainer to silence width(-1) warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py crossed 1100 lines this iteration (new share, public, vault, public-image, estimate, primary-image, delete-image handlers). Strongly recommend splitting into share_router.py / public_router.py / images_router.py.

/api/public/image reads the entire user's cards collection on every vault-token image hit to build allowed_paths (to_list(5000)). For users with large vaults this is O(N) per image request — cache allowed_paths per token with short TTL, or index on (user_id, image_path).

public_card endpoint leaks owner.user_id inside sanitized card? Actually _sanitize_card_public does not include user_id — good. But public_vault_token is still embedded in owner dict which is fine (it IS the token used).

share_token format c_<hex16> and vault token v_<hex16> — 64 bits of entropy, acceptable for obscurity-style sharing but not cryptographic. OK for this use case; consider documenting it as 'unlisted link' not 'secret'.
estimate_price uses session_id=f'est-{user_id}-{item_id}' which means repeated estimates on the same item share LlmChat session state. Since send_message is one-shot this is benign, but worth noting.
DELETE /api/cards/{id}/image marks file is_deleted but does not remove from S3/Mongo storage — orphan accumulation (same concern as avatar rotation from iter 3).
public_image returns 404 when a file was never stored in db.files. If file exists in storage but not in db.files (legacy), it will 404. Consistent with /files serving, good.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_4.py (new, 15 cases covering multi-image, share/revoke, public card/vault, public-image path-scope, AI estimate)

success_rate.backend: 100% (50/51 pass, 1 skipped due to LLM budget exhaustion mid-test — not a code defect)

seed_data_creation: All TEST_I4_* cards + watch items cleaned up. Public vault disabled and card shares revoked at end of test run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 4 adds share/public routes: POST/DELETE /api/cards/{id}/share, POST /api/users/me/public-vault?enabled=, GET /api/public/card/{token}, GET /api/public/vault/{token}, GET /api/public/image/{token}/{path:path}, PUT /api/cards/{id}/primary-image?path=, POST /api/cards/{id}/image?replace=false, POST /api/watchlist/{id}/estimate. All public endpoints sanitize cost/sales fields (price_paid/price_sold/expenses/where_bought). public-image has path-scope enforcement (share_token → only that card's paths; vault_token → only that user's cards + avatar_path). Frontend routes /s/c/:token and /s/v/:token render unauthenticated. MultiImageManager (testids manager-img-*, manager-primary-*, manager-delete-*, manager-add), Lightbox (lightbox, lightbox-close, lightbox-next, lightbox-prev), CollectionCard share menu (more-card-*, share-copy-*, share-open-*, share-revoke-*, card-shared-* badge), Watchlist (comps-link-*, estimate-button-*, ai-estimate-*), Profile (public-vault-toggle, public-vault-link, copy-vault-link, open-vault-link), PublicCard (public-card-page, public-card-image, public-card-tags), PublicVault (public-vault-page, public-card-*). AI estimate currently works but EMERGENT_LLM_KEY has a very low budget cap ($0.001) — expect intermittent 502s until topped up.
rca of the issue: Sole anomaly: 502 on /watchlist/{id}/estimate during pytest run caused by EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget exceeded ('Current cost: 0.001755, Max budget: 0.001'). Root cause: LiteLLM budget cap on the test key, not app code. Mitigation: the endpoint correctly catches the LiteLLM exception, logs it, and returns HTTP 502 as specified. Frontend run earlier (before budget ran out) confirmed real Claude output $180–$280 typical $225 with note about iconic rookie, matching the sample response quoted by the main agent. No code fix required.
